sources.citation: MDN Web Docs. HTML: HyperText Markup Language.

learning_objectives: L1: Define HTML and explain its role alongside CSS and JavaScript.
L1: Identify the required elements of a valid HTML5 document.
L1: Name and describe: h1–h6, p, a, img, ul, ol, li, div, span.
L2: Explain block-level vs inline elements with examples.
L2: Describe how src and href attributes work.
L2: Explain the parent-child relationship in the HTML DOM tree.
L3: Write a valid HTML5 page with heading, paragraph, image, link, and list.
L3: Debug an HTML snippet with a missing closing tag or broken image path.

concepts.title: HTML, CSS, and JavaScript
concepts.explanation: HTML: structure and content. CSS: visual presentation. JavaScript: interactivity. HTML is always required.
concepts.worked_example: News article: HTML=headline+paragraphs+image. CSS=fonts/layout. JS=carousel. Remove CSS: plain but readable. Remove HTML: nothing renders.

concepts.title: HTML5 Document Structure
concepts.explanation: <!DOCTYPE html> → HTML5. <html lang='en'> → root. <head> → metadata (not visible). <body> → all visible content. <meta charset='UTF-8'> → correct rendering.

concepts.title: Headings, Paragraphs, Text
concepts.explanation:
–

: h1 most important (one per page).

: paragraph. : bold semantic. : italic. Never use headings for visual size only — use CSS.
concepts.worked_example:
Web Dev

Getting Started

HTML is the foundation of every web page.

concepts.title: Block vs Inline
concepts.explanation: Block: new line, full width — div, h1–h6, p, ul, ol. Inline: flows in text — span, a, strong, em, img.

concepts.title: HTML Forms
concepts.explanation: <form action='/submit' method='post'>. GET: URL/searches. POST: body/sensitive data. Inputs: text, email, password, checkbox, radio, submit. <label for='id'>: links to input for accessibility.
concepts.worked_example: <form action='/login' method='post'><label for='e'>Email:</label><input type='email' id='e' name='email' required><input type='submit' value='Log In'></form>

concepts.title: Semantic HTML5
concepts.explanation: <header>, <nav>, <main> (one per page), <section>, <article>, <aside>, <footer>. Benefits: accessibility, SEO, clean code.
concepts.worked_example: Non-semantic: <div id='header'>. Semantic: <header><nav>...</nav></header><main>...</main>. Screen readers announce landmarks.
